purpose: Define the production-domain PWA installation, offline, update, and authenticated mobile-web checks that must pass before using the public PWA as an external pilot channel.

officialBasis.name: MDN - Making PWAs installable
officialBasis.localRequirement: Verify installable manifest, icons, HTTPS origin, install prompt behavior, and standalone launch.

officialBasis.name: MDN - Service Worker API
officialBasis.localRequirement: Verify cached shell, offline fallback, update lifecycle, and no mutation API caching.

officialBasis.name: web.dev - Define your app icons
officialBasis.localRequirement: Keep 192px, 512px, and maskable icons available for install surfaces.

publicAssetSmokeEvidence.conclusion: Production PWA public assets are reachable and structurally valid on the public HTTPS domain. Device install, offline relaunch, update cycle, and authenticated session evidence still require real device or review-account validation.

deviceInstallEvidenceTemplate.purpose: Record sanitized Android Chrome and iOS Safari install, home-screen launch, offline, update, and safe-area evidence before expanding PWA pilot usage.

expectations: at least 192, 512, and maskable icons are present
shortcuts include create, assets, and settings
message event can trigger SKIP_WAITING for user-confirmed updates
offline state is clear to non-technical users
page can be served when navigation fetch fails
maskable icon loads from production origin

deviceMatrix.label: Android Chrome
mustPass: beforeinstallprompt is shown after user engagement
Install button opens browser install UI
Installed app launches standalone from home screen
Bottom navigation, beginner cards, and notification card fit the screen
Login/session works against the production origin
Offline relaunch shows cached shell or offline page
A deployed update shows the in-app update banner and reloads cleanly

deviceMatrix.label: iOS Safari
mustPass: Safari guidance card tells user to use Share then Add to Home Screen
Added home-screen app launches standalone or browser-compatible view
Safe-area spacing does not overlap iOS bars
Login/session works against the production origin
Offline relaunch shows cached shell or offline page
Public support, privacy, AI disclosure, and account deletion pages open

deviceMatrix.label: Desktop Chrome installability
mustPass: Manifest and service worker are recognized
Install entry is available when Chrome considers the app installable
Desktop installed window still presents the production console correctly

steps: Run npm run public:check against https://www.zkraiflow.top.
Confirm manifest, service worker, offline page, icons, legal pages, and health check load.
manualTestCases.expectedResult: Public PWA assets and legal/support pages load from HTTPS with no blocking errors.

steps: Wait for the in-app install banner or browser install entry.
Tap install, complete browser install, and launch from the home screen.
manualTestCases.expectedResult: The app opens as an installed standalone experience and starts on the beginner-friendly mobile home.

steps: Confirm the app shows the add-to-home-screen guidance card.
Use Share > Add to Home Screen, then launch from the home screen.
manualTestCases.expectedResult: The app opens from the iOS home screen without layout overlap or missing public pages.

steps: Launch the PWA once online and open the home, create, assets, and settings routes.
Relaunch and navigate to a previously opened route.
manualTestCases.expectedResult: The cached shell or offline page is shown clearly; create/generation actions do not pretend to run while offline.

steps: Install or open the current PWA.
Deploy a version with a changed service worker cache version.
Reload once, wait for update detection, then tap the in-app update banner.
manualTestCases.expectedResult: The update banner appears only when a new worker is waiting, and tapping it activates the new worker and reloads once.

steps: Log in with the review-safe account on the production origin.
Create a dry-run task, open assets/results, and open support/legal/data rights pages.
Close and relaunch the installed PWA.
manualTestCases.expectedResult: Session behavior, secure cookies, CORS, and protected API calls remain stable after relaunch.
